data.title: Blackstone among suitors for UnitedHealth-owned Optum UK - report
data.description:
• Blackstone (NYSE:BX) is among a group of private equity firms considering offers for Optum''s UK operations, which is owned by UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH).
  • Bank of America has been hired to advise on the sale process, according to a  Sky News report on Friday. Several other large PE firms are in the early stage of considering bids for the UK business.
  • Optum told Sky News it doesn't comment on rumors or speculation. Blackstone declined to comment.

data.title: Consumer sentiment softens in second October print as long-run inflation outlook heats up
data.description:
Consumer Sentiment Index: 53.6 vs. the initial estimate of 55.0 and 55.1 in September, according to University of Michigan Survey of Consumers data released on Friday.

Current conditions index: 58.6 vs. the prior October print of 61.0 and 60.4 in September.

Consumer expectations: 50.3 vs. initial estimate of 51.2 and September's 51.7 reading.

Year-ahead inflation expectations: +4.6%, unchanged from the initial October reading, edged down from +4.7% in September.

Long-run inflation expectations: +3.9% vs. +3.7% in October's initial read and +3.7% in the previous month. 

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Biogen, Vanqua ink licensing deal worth up to $1B for immunology drug
data.description:
• Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) on Friday announced a licensing agreement worth up to $1B to obtain exclusive global rights to an immunology candidate developed by Chicago-based neuro drug developer Vanqua Bio.
  • The oral preclinical candidate is designed to target C5aR1, a cellular receptor involved in neutrophil-mediated inflammation, which is linked to multiple inflammatory disorders, said Biogen’s (NASDAQ:BIIB) head of research, Jane Grogan.
  • As part of the deal, Vanqua will receive $70M upfront in addition to milestone payments worth up to $990M as well as tiered royalties on potential net sales. Cambridge, Massachusetts-based Biogen (BIIB) will lead the future development, production, and commercialization of the drug candidate.
  • Biogen (BIIB) will also record acquired in-process R&D expense in Q4 2025 as a result of the transaction.

data.title: Citi turns bullish on MNTN stock as risk/reward scenario appears 'compelling'
data.description:
Shares of adtech firm MNTN Inc. (NYSE:MNTN) rose 13% in early open market trading on Friday after Citi Research upgraded the company to "buy" from a previous investment rating of "hold," citing a compelling risk/reward scenario at current levels.

The research firm believes that the company's CTV targeting and creative tools like MNTN Matched and the upcoming launch of QuickFrame AI can deliver continued PTV customer growth as margins expand. 

"A key differentiator at MNTN, in our view, are its direct relationships with its PTV customers, and as ROAS improves, we believe MNTN is well positioned to gain a greater share of its customers' budgets," Citi said in its research note dated October 24. 

For the third quarter, Citi forecasts MNTN's total revenue to rise 23% from last year to $70.3M, in line with consensus. On profitability, they project adj. EBITDA of $14.3M (20.4% margin), also in line with consensus.

MNTN's price target was lowered by $11 to $22, now implying an upside of 37.4%.

data.title: General Dynamics stock rises to record high on Q3 beat
data.description:
General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) on Friday rose as much as 5.6% to touch a record high after the aerospace-and-defense company topped Wall Street’s third-quarter expectations on both profit and sales, powered by robust Gulfstream jet deliveries and steady demand across its defense units.

 

The Reston, Va.-based contractor reported net income of $1.06 billion, or $3.88 a share, up from $930 million, or $3.35 a share, a year earlier, on revenue of $12.9 billion, a 10.6% increase, topping Wall Street’s estimates for earnings of $3.72 a share and revenue of $12.53 billion.

What drove the beat

  • Aerospace standout: Segment revenue jumped 30.3% to $3.23 billion with 39 Gulfstream deliveries (up from 28 a year ago), lifting margins to 13.3%. Management cited “very strong” order activity for business jets.
  • Defense steady: Marine Systems revenue rose 13.8%; Combat Systems edged up 1.8%; Technologies was roughly flat. Companywide operating margin expanded to 10.3%.

Orders, cash and backlog

  • Orders: $19.3 billion in the quarter for a book-to-bill of 1.5 times (defense 1.6 times; aerospace 1.3 times).
  • Backlog/TPV: $109.9 billion in backlog; $167.7 billion including estimated potential contract value on IDIQs and options.
  • Cash flow: $2.1 billion from operations (199% of net earnings); free cash flow $1.9 billion

data.title: Financial Institutions, Inc. raises 2025 ROAA and ROAE targets amid strong loan growth and margin expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Financial Institutions, Inc. (FISI) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Martin Birmingham stated that the company "reported strong third quarter 2025 financial results marked by balance sheet growth, robust revenue generation, improved profitability metrics and meaningful build of tangible and regulatory capital." He highlighted a 1.2% loan growth driven by commercial lending in Upstate New York and a 3.9% increase in total deposits, noting support from seasonal public deposit inflows and growth in core commercial and consumer deposits.
  • Birmingham announced upward revisions to full year 2025 guidance: "We now expect ROAA for the year to exceed 115 basis points, up from our previous guide of 110 basis points and an ROAE of greater than 12%, up from 11.25%." He also indicated the company expects to achieve the high end of its original 1% to 3% loan growth range.
  • CFO Jack Plants remarked, "Net interest margin expanded 16 basis points on a linked-quarter basis, reflective of improved yields on average earning assets alongside deposit repricing that supported reduced funding costs." He highlighted a net interest income of $51.8 million, up $2.7 million from the previous quarter, and noninterest income of $12.1 million, up $1.4 million from the last quarter. Plants also discussed tightening full year net interest margin guidance to between 350 and 355 basis points and noted, "We are narrowing the range for our expected effective tax rate to between 18% to 19% for 2025."

Outlook

  • Birmingham stated, "We now expect ROAA for the year to exceed 115 basis points...and an ROAE of greater than 12%." He added that loan growth is expected at the high end of the 1%-3% range, with particular strength in commercial lending.
  • Plants provided guidance for a full-year net interest margin of 350 to 355 basis points, with "modest margin pressure in the fourth quarter" but anticipated improvement in 2026 driven by loan growth and cost management. He also stated, "We remain comfortable with our guided full year net charge-off ratio range of between 25 and 35 basis points."

Financial Results

  • Net income available to common shareholders was $20.1 million, or $0.99 per diluted share, for the third quarter. Return on average assets was 132 basis points, and return on average equity was 13.31%.
  • Total deposits at period end reached $5.36 billion, up 3.9% from the previous quarter. Commercial loans stood at about $3 billion, an increase of 1.6% from the prior quarter and 8.3% year-over-year. Consumer indirect balances were $838.7 million, up 0.6% from June 30 but down 4.1% year-over-year.
  • Annualized net charge-offs to average loans were 18 basis points for the quarter. Noninterest expense was $35.9 million, up slightly due to higher medical claims, with full year expenses expected to be about $141 million. The efficiency ratio was about 58%, below the 60% target.

Q&A

  • Damon Del Monte, KBW: Asked about margin outlook with potential rate cuts. Birmingham responded, "Our guided range that we provided for full year margin...would have -- a rate cut would have a modest impact to the full year guide. We'd still hold on that guidance potentially at the bottom end of the range. But I would expect that going into 2026, our jumping off point would probably be somewhere around 3.60%."
  • Del Monte: Inquired about increased buyback activity. Birmingham stated, "We're pleased that our Board approved the buyback. It's another option that we have to support the shares and invest in ourselves, and we look forward to updating the market...when activity occurs."
  • Del Monte: Questioned optimism for loan growth into 2026. CFO Plants said, "Certainly, our experience we've had lately at the tail end of 2025 has been encouraging. I think that high -- or mid-single-digit growth, as you can is appropriate for modeling purposes."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts asked pointed questions about margin sustainability, capital deployment through buybacks, and the trajectory of loan growth, indicating a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought clarity on management's increased optimism.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, especially in the prepared remarks, using phrases such as "We now expect," "We remain comfortable," and "We are narrowing the range," reflecting growing confidence in the company's financial trajectory.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management appear more upbeat, with analysts probing for confirmation of improved outlook and management signaling stronger guidance and optimism for future results.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance was raised for ROAA and ROAE, up from 110 basis points to over 115 and from 11.25% to over 12%, respectively. Net income and profitability metrics improved over the previous quarter.
  • Management tone shifted from cautious affirmation of guidance in Q2 to more confident upward revisions and an optimistic outlook for 2026.
  • Analyst focus shifted from verifying stability and expense control in Q2 to exploring further upside and capital deployment options in Q3.
  • The company signaled incremental improvement in returns and highlighted continued strong commercial lending momentum, particularly in Upstate New York.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted increased nonperforming loans, but clarified that the $1.5 million increase was due to "4 smaller commercial loan downgrades, each in different industries and geographies facing unique issues."
  • Credit discipline and diversification were emphasized, with nonperforming loans ratio at 74 basis points, down from 94 basis points a year ago.
  • Expense pressures were noted in self-funded medical plan claims, with expectations for this to remain elevated in Q4, but offset by cost controls elsewhere.
  • The company continues to wind down its BaaS platform, with related deposits expected to flow off by early 2026.

Final Takeaway

Management at Financial Institutions, Inc. highlighted robust third quarter results, raising full-year profitability guidance and expressing increased optimism for 2026. Strong commercial loan growth, expanding net interest margin, and disciplined expense management were underscored, while credit quality and capital ratios remain healthy. The company expects to maintain momentum into year-end, targeting incremental improvement in returns and operational efficiency.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Grindr surges after holders offer $18/share in take private
data.description:
• Grindr (NYSE:GRND) jumped 25% after shareholders offered to buy the dating app for $18 a share. 
  • The holders, who own more than 60% of the outstanding shares, offered to buy Grindr (GRND) for $18 a share, according to a  letter the holders wrote to the Grindr board on Friday. The preliminary and non-binding proposal comes from Ray Zage and James Lu, who are both board members.
  • The potential buyers said they will fund the acquisition with a combination of equity and debt financing. The shareholders also said they have secured significant expressions of interest for financing, including multiple highly confident letters.
  • Earlier this month, Grindr (NYSE:GRND) confirmed that its board received a letter from large holders exploring the potential of purchasing the dating app.

data.title: CPI in charts: Annual core rate comes in at 3.0% in September
U.S. PMI Composite edges up as manufacturing, services PMIs surprise higher
data.description: The S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI increased to 54.8 in October's flash estimate from 53.9 in September, according to data released on Friday.

data.title: Sagimet rises after denifanstat succeeds in late stage trial
data.description:
• Sagimet Biosciences (NASDAQ:SGMT) announced positive results from a late-stage trial of denifanstat, a treatment for moderate to severe acne vulgaris, which was conducted by its partner, Ascletis Bioscience, in China.
  • The trial successfully achieved all primary and secondary objectives.
  • At the 12-week mark, patients treated with denifanstat experienced treatment success rates that were more than double those of the placebo group, showing significant improvements in both inflammatory and non-inflammatory acne lesions.
  • Overall, denifanstat was well tolerated by participants. Following the announcement, SGMT stock rose approximately 6.3%.
